summary: The second mesiobuccal canal is typically not visible by naked eye due to limited anatomical exposure; studies suggest it may only be observable in approximately 62-70% of cases when relying solely on unaided vision. This range accounts for natural variation in root anatomy and inherent difficulties in achieving adequate visualization during standard procedures.
